name: 120 S W C Owen Avenue storefront strip
style: one-story multi-tenant storefront strip with a shallow overhanging eave, blue roofline trim, cream upper walls, dark lower panels, and varied tenant bays

floors.windowPattern: alternating large dark storefront windows and narrow glazed entry bays; the extreme endpoints are cropped

notes: A pink-painted tenant bay is partly visible at the left end; cream and dark gray are the predominant colors across the documented frontage.
Opening totals remain unset because both façade endpoints are cropped.
The perpendicular daylight view and footprint geometry provide a strong match.
1752189312034418.jpg depicts adjacent building ends and is not used as evidence for this descriptor.

name: 501 E Sugarland Highway auto-service strip
style: one-story multi-tenant auto-service strip with burgundy, navy, and pale-gray storefront sections, a black-and-white checker accent band, and an open vehicle service passage

floors.windowPattern: small storefront glazing and service-office openings distributed among differently colored tenant bays; endpoints and several openings are obscured

notes: A palm trunk blocks the center-left frontage and the image crops both ends.
The checker pattern is concentrated along the lower wall and around the central service section.
The close perpendicular view matches the long irregular footprint; parcel-level attributes are not used because the parcel contains four buildings.
No business wording is retained because the building-mounted text is blurred or cropped.

name: Martinez Tires
style: one-story tire-service building with a tall painted parapet, deep corrugated-metal work canopy, white support posts, and multiple roll-up service bays

notes: The upper parapet is dusty beige-pink with dark reddish-brown lower wall bands.
Three complete roll-up doors are visible; an additional service area is cropped at the left edge and is not counted.
The five-meter perpendicular view gives a strong footprint and façade match.
Only the three fully visible roll-up bays are encoded.

name: 500 S Francisco Street manufactured home
style: single-wide manufactured home with pale aqua siding, white skirting and trim, a very shallow roof, and small irregularly spaced side openings

floors.windowPattern: at least two small rectangular windows, including one fitted with a window air-conditioning unit; the right end is obscured by vegetation

notes: White metal skirting runs along the full visible base.
A tree and palmetto obscure the right portion, so the total window count is left unset.
The narrow footprint dimensions, close perpendicular view, and matching long-side orientation provide a strong match.
The detached Everglades sign is not building signage.
552358074494978.jpg is too dark to add details, and 493362977076582.jpg does not target this footprint.
